author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
2026-06-23: Ticket 1 of 8 shipped (`eb42f50 v3-write-path`).
Landed as commit `74827e93`: new `git.rs` + `v3_write.rs`
modules, mode-gated dispatch at `Storage::open` (presence of
`refs/jjf/meta/format-version` selects v3). V3 path uses
`git mktree` + `git commit-tree` + `git update-ref` CAS — zero
jj subprocess calls, git HEAD never moves. 431 tests green
(421 + 10 new v3 integration tests).

V2 path is fully preserved (no migration in this ticket).
Ticket `6e2c843` (read path, next in chain) is unblocked.

Surprise from the subagent worth carrying to ticket 2:
the snapshot cache (`cache.rs`) is v2-only; `Storage::read`,
`list_ready`, `list_memories`, `resolve` all go through it.
On a v3-shape repo those public methods don't work yet.
Ticket 2's read-path repointing needs to subsume the cache
or coordinate explicitly with ticket 6 (cache port). Worth
flagging in the dispatch prompt.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
2026-06-23: Ticket 2 of 8 shipped (`6e2c843 v3-read-path`).
Landed as commit `f7fe0061`: V3 reads route through git-CLI
(`cat_blob`, `walk_commits`, `for_each_ref`) when
`StorageMode::V3`. V2 reads unchanged. 13 new tests
(444 total, was 431). Debug-build op-replay cross-check
ports to V3 with `should_panic` injected-divergence test.

Decision: V3 reads bypass the snapshot cache (option A per
ticket scope). Ticket 6 (`aa915fe v3-cache`) carries the
cache port. Cost: N git calls per `list_ready` until
ticket 6 — sub-second on ~1k issues, fine.

Ticket 3 (`add0646 v3-init`, next in chain) is unblocked.

Notes from the subagent for downstream tickets:
- Ticket 3: can call `v3_write::write_format_version_sentinel`
  directly (already exists from ticket 1).
- Ticket 4 (migrator): can reuse `v3_write::commit_record_v3`
  per replay step; `history::read_history_at` (v2) gives the
  source op chain.
- Ticket 6 (cache): key shape is `for_each_ref` shas
  concatenated; existing `cache::load_or_rebuild` ports
  cleanly. `read_history_at(rev, id)` is still V2-only —
  V3's "rev" doesn't translate to a bookmark revset, so any
  future merge-driver work needs to re-think this.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
2026-06-24: Tickets 3 of 8 shipped (`add0646 v3-init`).
Landed as commit `5c6cc54d`: fresh repos init to V3-shape
(plant `refs/jjf/meta/format-version` sentinel via
`v3_write::write_format_version_sentinel`, no bookmark, no
working-copy mutation). V2 init paths idempotent for existing
v2 repos.

**Bug surfaced and filed: `a6b8fb7 v3-mutate-recheck`**
(typed bug). Root cause: `Storage::mutate_once` re-reads the
record on CAS retry but blindly re-runs the user closure
WITHOUT rechecking domain preconditions. Two parallel `jjf
ready --claim` racers both pass the pre-`mutate` Open-status
check, both call mutate, the loser retries with a stale
operation against a freshly-read InProgress record, lands a
duplicate trailer, exit 0.

Bug exists in V2 too — V2's bookmark-CAS broadened the race
window enough that the test presented as a timing flake.
V3's per-issue-ref CAS narrows the window and exposes it
deterministically.

**Per CLAUDE.md's bugs-before-features rule, `a6b8fb7`
ships next.** Tickets 4–8 in the v3 epic chain wait. The
parallel-claim test (`parallel_ready_claim_limit_one_assigns_two_different_ids`)
is the acceptance smoke. Current main has 445/446 passing
with that one test known-failing.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
2026-06-24: Bug `a6b8fb7 v3-mutate-recheck` shipped
(commit `19fb6237`). Tri-state `MutateOutcome::{Write,
Skip, Conflict}` return from the mutate closure; domain
preconditions for `claim`, `unclaim`, `block`, `unblock`,
`update` (slug uniqueness), `add_dep_edge` (target
existence) all re-evaluate on CAS-loss retry. New
`ClaimResult::{Claimed, AlreadyOurs}` lets `ready --claim`
surface a typed `claim_race_lost` envelope (exit 1) when
the parallel race is lost.

Repro test
`parallel_ready_claim_limit_one_assigns_two_different_ids`
passes 5/5 deterministically. 452/452 workspace tests
green (+6 new tests).

V2 path inherited the same fix (the bug was in the retry
contract, not the storage shape) — V2's flake should be
gone too, though V2-specific verification needs a v2-shape
fixture planter (out of scope; ticket 4's migrator is a
natural place to clean that up).

Bug queue is clear. V3 chain resumes at `c14e1c1
v3-migrator` next.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
2026-06-24: Ticket 4 of 8 shipped (`c14e1c1 v3-migrator`).
Landed as commit `1541f4b0`: new `migrate_v2_v3.rs` module
+ `Storage::open` wiring. Walks the v2 op chain per issue
via `jj log` (path-filtered on v2 and v1 paths), rebuilds
each commit on `refs/jjf/issues/<id>` byte-identically
(snapshot bytes + verbatim message). Memories follow the
same shape on `refs/jjf/memories/<key>`. Bookmark deleted
last; sentinel planted as idempotency marker. Per-issue
idempotency on ref-already-exists.

458 tests green (+6 new v2→v3 migration tests).

**Scaffold worth knowing about:** the subagent added a
test-only `JJF_DISABLE_V2_TO_V3_MIGRATION` env var
(checked in `Storage::open` at lib.rs:974) so v2-internals
tests can keep working through tickets 5 and 7. The env
var is set in `make_scratch_repo`, `push_pull.rs::run_jjf`,
and the v2→v3 migration test itself. **Ticket 7 (test
sweep) MUST delete both the env-var check and the
test-helper sets**; ticket 5 (push/pull) needs to either
preserve the env-var path or migrate to v3 refspecs
simultaneously with the test fixtures.

V3 chain continues at ticket 5 (`07c1dc9 v3-push-pull`).

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
2026-06-24: Ticket 5 of 8 shipped (`07c1dc9 v3-push-pull`).
Landed as commit `5c41d59b`: new `sync_v3.rs` module + CLI
push/pull rewrites. Push runs `git push <remote>
'refs/jjf/*:refs/jjf/*'`; pull runs `git fetch <remote>
'refs/jjf/*:refs/remotes/<remote>/jjf/*'` then applies the
five-scenario per-ref merge (new, identical, local-ahead,
fast-forward, diverged). Diverged refs land a 2-parent
commit with the LWW-resolved snapshot in tree and
`Jjf-Op: merge` trailer.

15 push/pull tests covering single-clone round-trip,
same-field LWW divergence, different-fields union,
independent-issue FF, sentinel bootstrap, preflight
errors. 460/460 workspace tests green.

**Scaffold update:** ticket 5 took Option A — dropped the
`JJF_DISABLE_V2_TO_V3_MIGRATION` env-var set from
`push_pull.rs::run_jjf`. The migrator is a no-op on
fresh-init v3 fixtures, so the env var wasn't actually
load-bearing there. **Ticket 7 still owns deleting the
env var entirely** (both the check in lib.rs and the
sets in `make_scratch_repo` + the migration test).

**Side-confirmation worth noting:** the subagent verified
v3 push/pull does NOT drift git HEAD from inside the
source repo. The self-host guard is now empirically
unnecessary for v3 — ticket 8 will remove it.

V3 chain continues at ticket 6 (`aa915fe v3-cache`).

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
2026-06-24: Ticket 6 of 8 shipped (`aa915fe v3-cache`).
Landed as commit `58789ee5`: cache key for V3 is the SHA-1
of sorted `<refname> <objectname>\n` lines for
`refs/jjf/issues/*` + `refs/jjf/memories/*` (sentinel
excluded). Cache file format has a `format_kind: "v2" |
"v3"` discriminator; V3 repos rebuild on first read; V2
warm caches preserved. All V3 read paths (`read`,
`list_ids`, `list_ready`, `list_memories`, `read_memory`,
`resolve`, `dep_tree`, `find_open_slug_collision`) wired
through the cache.

462/462 workspace tests green. New tests cover V3
cache-hit-avoids-rebuild and V3-mutation-invalidates-cache.

**Note for ticket 7 (test sweep):** the prior
`v3_replay_panics_on_injected_file_divergence` test was
renamed to `v3_read_returns_cached_record_after_ref_tamper`
and `#[should_panic]` dropped — because cached reads now
shadow ref tampers (cache key is ref-sha derived). The
debug-build cross-check still fires on cache-miss-for-id
(write-path bugs surfacing same-process). Ticket 7 may
want a dedicated cache-miss tamper test.

V3 chain continues at ticket 7 (`53f9728 v3-test-sweep`).

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
2026-06-24: Ticket 7 of 8 shipped (`53f9728 v3-test-sweep`).
Landed as commit `dfb17f52`: workspace tests ported to v3
ref-shape assertions. Specifically:

- Deleted `JJF_DISABLE_V2_TO_V3_MIGRATION` env var entirely.
  Replaced with a `Storage::open_skip_v2_to_v3_migration`
  test-only constructor for the migration tests' planted-v2
  fixtures. Grep audit: zero hits across the codebase.
- ~30 tests in `integration.rs` ported from `bookmarks(issues)`
  / `jj file show` to `refs/jjf/issues/<id>:issue.json` via
  `git cat-file blob`. Memory and trailer-block tests ported
  similarly.
- New `v3_no_head_drift_across_full_mutation_matrix` test in
  `v3_write_path.rs` covering claim/unclaim, block/unblock,
  label add/rm, dep add/rm (default + typed parent-child).
- Fixed v3 timestamp regression in `git::walk_commits`: was
  emitting TZ offset (`-04:00`); now uses `%ad` with
  `--date=format-local:%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ` to match the
  historical Z-form contract.

463/463 workspace tests green.

**For ticket 8:** the subagent noted that "4-CLI dance" /
"v2 dance" references remain in code COMMENTS (in lib.rs,
preflight.rs). They no longer correspond to runtime
behavior. Ticket 8 should scrub these alongside the
self-host guard removal.

V3 chain finishes at ticket 8 (`52e5c49 v3-guard-removal`)
— the celebratory delete that closes `08cf14b`.
